cluster_properties.redundancy: LOW — each lesson measures a different subsystem (filter cascade, INDEX pointers, quality scoring, signal routing, external grounding)
cluster_properties.existing_coverage: No L4 parent. L-926 (namespace disconnection) is nearest but covers only domain-global linkage, not the general retention/access split. L-913 (overconfidence equilibrium) touches measurement blindness but not storage/access divergence.

expect: Cross-domain distillation of retention-vs-accessibility cluster produces L3+ lesson. Unifying mechanism: storage completeness diverges from access fidelity as N grows. 4 domains (filtering/brain/evaluation/expert-swarm).
actual: L-1096 written (L3, Sh=9). Unifying mechanism confirmed: 5 independent findings across 4 domains all show storage metrics at 94-100% masking access metrics at 0-31%. Gap ranges 16-100pp. Common cause: retention is passive (append-only naturally retains) while accessibility requires active infrastructure (routing, indexing, targeting) that must scale with N. Rule: for any measurement, name both storage and access metrics; if only one exists, the missing one is the bottleneck.
diff: Expected L3+ from cross-domain cluster: MET. The cluster satisfied all positive criteria (multi-domain, no L4 parent, identifiable cross-lesson mechanism) and avoided all negative criteria from S448 boundary condition (not single-domain, not instances of existing L4, pattern not previously named). Distillation produced genuinely novel architectural insight — the storage/access divergence is not named in any prior lesson.
note: n=7 with 71% L3+ rate (5/7). Baseline threshold 15% — exceeded by 4.7x. Recovery from S448 falsification dip (67%) to 71%. Cross-domain cluster with low redundancy and no L4 parent produced L3 as predicted. Boundary condition from S448 holds: clusters WITH these properties succeed; clusters WITHOUT (single-domain, high-redundancy, existing L4 parent) fail. Target: n=10 for CONFIRMED.
